data.title: BeOne Medicines Sets 2026 AGM and Moves to Web-First Proxy Distribution
data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BeOne Medicines Ltd ( (HK:6160) ) has provided an update.

BeOne Medicines Ltd. has convened its 2026 annual general meeting of shareholders for June 11, 2026, in Zurich, setting May 22, 2026, as the record date for eligibility to vote. The company, which is listed in the U.S., Hong Kong and Shanghai, is coordinating the meeting across these markets to accommodate its broad shareholder base and regulatory obligations.

The group will primarily distribute proxy materials and its 2025 annual report via the internet, with printed copies going only to STAR Market RMB-shareholders or investors who request them or where local rules require. This digital-first approach is intended to streamline access for holders of Swiss-registered shares, Hong Kong–listed shares and Nasdaq-traded ADSs, while providing clear instructions on voting, meeting participation and access to financial documents.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:6160) stock is a Buy with a HK$290.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BeOne Medicines Ltd stock, see the HK:6160 Stock Forecast page.

More about BeOne Medicines Ltd

BeOne Medicines Ltd. is a biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq, the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and Shanghai’s STAR Market, with its operations and shareholder register anchored in Switzerland. The company targets global capital markets through ordinary shares and ADSs to fund its research-driven medicines portfolio and engage a geographically diverse investor base.

data.title: GCL New Energy Raises HK$131 Million Through Share Placement for Solar Expansion
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

GCL New Energy Holdings ( (HK:0451) ) has issued an update.

GCL New Energy Holdings has completed a share placement of 127 million new shares, representing about 7.55% of its enlarged issued share capital, at HK$1.05 per share to at least six independent investors. None of the placees or their associates became substantial shareholders as a result of the transaction, preserving the existing shareholder structure.

The placement raised gross proceeds of approximately HK$133 million, with net proceeds of about HK$131 million after expenses. The company plans to allocate HK$60 million to expand and digitally upgrade its solar operations and management business, HK$15 million to grow its energy and related products trading business, HK$15 million to repay bank and other loans, and HK$41 million for general working capital, with most funds expected to be fully deployed by the end of 2026.

More about GCL New Energy Holdings

GCL New Energy Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the solar energy and broader clean energy sector. Its primary businesses include solar operations and management, as well as trading of energy and related products, with a focus on expanding and digitally upgrading its renewable energy capabilities.

YT Realty Group ( (HK:0075) ) has shared an announcement.

Y.T. Realty Group has convened its next annual general meeting for 10 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider the re-election of one executive director and one independent non-executive director, along with approving directors’ and auditors’ remuneration and the re-appointment of Ernst & Young. Investors will also vote on renewing mandates that allow the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s shares and to issue new shares and related securities, measures that maintain capital management flexibility and support the company’s ability to respond to financing and strategic opportunities in the property market.

The proposed share repurchase mandate would enable Y.T. Realty Group to buy back a limited portion of its issued share capital, potentially supporting the share price and capital efficiency. The general mandate to allot and issue additional shares, including via instruments such as warrants and convertible securities, ensures the board can quickly access equity capital if needed, balancing shareholder dilution risks with the capacity to fund growth or restructuring in a volatile real estate environment.

data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H ( (HK:2429) ).

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. has renewed and expanded its cooperation with Alipay.com by signing a new Payment Services Framework Agreement and an Advertising Cooperation Framework Agreement, effective from January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2028. Under these arrangements, Alipay will continue to provide payment services enabling online transactions on UBOX’s vending machines, while both parties will collaborate on advertising and promoting Alipay’s payment products on the machines.

Because Alipay.com is an associate of a substantial shareholder of UBOX through Ant Group, the deals are classified as continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules. The size of the annual caps triggers reporting, announcement and annual review requirements, but the transactions are exempt from independent shareholders’ approval, streamlining compliance while formalizing a key strategic relationship in UBOX’s payments and advertising ecosystem.

More about Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. Class H

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. operates smart vending machines and related online transaction infrastructure in China, providing automated retail services that rely heavily on digital payment channels. The company partners with major payment platforms to facilitate seamless cashless transactions for customers using its vending network across various locations.

Riverine China Holdings Limited ( (HK:1417) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Riverine China Holdings Limited has revised the terms of reference of its Nomination Committee, originally established in 2017, to align with updated Hong Kong corporate governance requirements on board diversity and committee composition. These changes reinforce the company’s commitment to fair and transparent director appointments and to maintaining a diverse and independent board structure in line with the Corporate Governance Code. data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from BYD Co ( (HK:1211) ) is now available.

BYD Company Limited has formalized detailed implementation rules for its Nomination Committee, a special committee under the board of directors tasked with overseeing the selection and governance of directors, the president and other senior officers. The rules define the committee’s composition, requiring at least three directors with a majority of independent non-executive directors, gender diversity, and an independent non-executive director as chairman, with terms aligned to those of the board.

Operational procedures are set out for convening meetings, voting, record-keeping and the use of teleconferencing and written resolutions, while ensuring adequate information and resources are provided to the committee. The Nomination Committee is mandated to annually review the board’s structure, size and skills mix, evaluate the independence of independent directors, and develop standards and procedures for senior appointments and succession planning, strengthening BYD’s corporate governance and board oversight as the company grows.

BYD Company Limited is a Chinese conglomerate operating primarily in the automotive and new energy sectors, best known globally for its electric vehicles and rechargeable batteries. The company has been expanding its international footprint while strengthening internal governance structures to support fast-growing operations and sustain competitiveness in global markets.

data.title: BYD’s First-Quarter Profit Slumps as Revenue and Cash Flow Weaken
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BYD Co ( (HK:1211) ) has issued an announcement.

BYD Company Limited reported a sharp downturn in first-quarter 2026 results, with operating revenue falling 11.82% year on year to RMB150.23 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders tumbling 55.38% to RMB4.08 billion. Earnings per share halved, return on net assets dropped to 1.65%, and operating cash flow slid 67.48%, though total assets and shareholders’ equity edged up slightly, reflecting balance-sheet expansion despite pressure on profitability.

The weaker performance underscores significant margin and cash flow compression for the group in the latest quarter, suggesting a more challenging operating environment after a period of rapid growth. While BYD’s asset and equity bases continued to grow modestly, the steep declines in profit and cash generation may sharpen investor focus on cost control, pricing power, and market dynamics in its core automotive and technology businesses.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1211) stock is a Buy with a HK$22.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BYD Co stock, see the HK:1211 Stock Forecast page.

More about BYD Co

BYD Company Limited is a Chinese joint stock company listed in Hong Kong and Shenzhen that operates in the automotive and related technology sectors. The group is known for its large-scale manufacturing capabilities and focuses on vehicles and associated products for both domestic and international markets.

Beijing UBOX Online Technology Corp. has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for May 28, 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review the company’s 2025 annual report, board work report, profit distribution plan, and consolidated financial statements, as well as the financial budget for 2026. Investors will also vote on reappointing PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor, adjusting remuneration for key executives including the chairman and CFO, withdrawing a prior CSRC application tied to a Pre-IPO incentive scheme, and granting the board a broad share issuance and treasury share disposal mandate that could influence future capital management and shareholder dilution.

The proposed general mandate to issue additional shares and deal with treasury shares aims to give the board more room to respond to financing needs or market opportunities within Hong Kong listing rules, while the withdrawal of the Pre-IPO share incentive application signals a shift in the company’s approach to equity-based compensation and regulatory engagement in mainland China.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Shenzhen Expressway Co ( (HK:0548) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shenzhen Expressway reported unaudited toll revenue figures for March 2026 across its portfolio of expressway projects, led by key assets such as the Outer Ring Project, Coastal Project and Jihe corridors in the Shenzhen region, as well as significant contributions from Qinglian and GS Expressways in other parts of Guangdong. The disclosure, which details revenues by project and average daily intake, is intended as interim statistical information, with the board cautioning investors that figures may differ from future audited data due to estimation methods and settlement adjustments, underscoring that the numbers should be used carefully for investment decisions.

The company also highlighted that some toll revenue data are affected by inter-network toll collection and subsequent reconciliations, meaning monthly forecasts may be revised as actual settlement data become available. This approach reflects both operational complexity in China’s toll road network and the company’s practice of updating forecasts, signaling that short-term revenue swings at the project level may not fully capture underlying long-term traffic and cash flow trends for stakeholders monitoring its performance.

More about Shenzhen Expressway Co

Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited is a toll road operator in the People’s Republic of China, focusing on the development, operation and management of expressways and related infrastructure. The company’s core assets are concentrated in Guangdong province, particularly in the Shenzhen region, with additional projects in other parts of Guangdong and several other Chinese provinces.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. ( (HK:1739) ) has shared an announcement.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. has replaced PricewaterhouseCoopers as its independent auditor after failing to reach an agreement on sharply higher audit fees linked to the group’s fast-growing international service operations. The board and audit committee determined that PwC’s proposed 2025 fee of RMB4 million, up from RMB2.8 million in 2024, exceeded the company’s budget, and invited tenders from other firms before selecting BDO with a proposal of RMB2.48 million.

The company emphasized that PwC’s resignation stemmed solely from the fee dispute rather than any issues with audit quality or governance, framing it as a mutually recognized impasse rather than a forced termination. BDO has since been appointed auditor and will conduct the 2025 audit under International Standards on Auditing using its global audit methodology, a move that may lower costs while maintaining compliance amid Qeeka’s growing international complexity.

More about Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc.

Qeeka Home (Cayman) Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed company that operates through a group structure and is incorporated in the Cayman Islands with limited liability. The group has been rapidly expanding its International Service Business, which has increased its operational scale, geographical reach and the complexity of its accounting and financial reporting requirements.

Shenzhen International Holdings ( (HK:0152) ) has provided an announcement.

Shenzhen International Holdings has released an overseas regulatory announcement disclosing the unaudited first quarterly report of 2026 for its subsidiary, Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited. The report, prepared under Chinese securities and stock exchange disclosure rules, covers the three months ended 31 March 2026 and is presented in RMB.

The board and senior management of Shenzhen Expressway collectively affirm the truthfulness, accuracy and completeness of the quarterly information and financial statements, while cautioning shareholders and public investors that the figures have not been audited. The filing meets Hong Kong and mainland regulatory requirements and underscores continued compliance and transparency obligations for investors in both markets.

More about Shenzhen International Holdings

Shenzhen International Holdings is an investment holding company with significant interests in infrastructure, including toll roads, through its subsidiary Shenzhen Expressway Corporation Limited. The group focuses on expressway operation, investment and management in mainland China, positioning itself as a key player in regional transport infrastructure and related services.
